title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience with deep specialization in AI search, ranking, recommendations, and personalization maps almost perfectly to this role's core charter. The Samsung.com work — hybrid search ranking, behavioral signals, precision@3 improvements, conversational AI, recommendation API, and A/B experimentation at 10M+ monthly users — directly mirrors what Airbnb's R&P team does. The one calibration: the role asks for 10+ years (Riley has 9) and the seniority signals lean toward a Staff/Lead PM level, making this a slight stretch, but the quality of Riley's portfolio more than compensates for the one-year delta.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain and archetype alignment — AI search, ranking, personalization, and discovery at scale are Riley's exact wheelhouse, with hard metrics to prove it.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving search relevance, personalization, and recommendation systems at global scale — including hybrid AI search across 3 international markets at Samsung.com and ML-driven recommendation platforms on a $2B+ GMV e-commerce platform. I specialize in partnering with ML engineers and applied researchers to ship ranking and discovery systems that measurably move bookings, engagement, and satisfaction metrics.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels — increasing NPS by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, boosting AOV by 15% — and built ML-driven recommendation systems at Kolonmall that grew AOV 25% on a $2B GMV platform
gap_handling: The 10+ year requirement is a soft gap (Riley has 9). Lean into the depth and scale of the Samsung and Amazon work rather than raw tenure — the precision@3, NPS, and AEO pipeline results signal Staff-level impact. On cover letter or in screening, frame the cross-marketplace scope of Samsung as equivalent to Airbnb's global marketplace complexity. Also note: the JD hints at marketplace fairness and host-side feedback loops — Riley should briefly reference the B2B seller platform work at Amazon as evidence of two-sided marketplace thinking.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core competencies — AI-powered discovery, search ranking, personalization, recommendation systems, and A/B experimentation at scale — map almost perfectly to what Airbnb is building on its homepage. The Samsung experience (10M+ monthly users, hybrid search, conversational AI, product discovery funnels) and the Kolon e-commerce background (recommendation systems, CTR optimization, conversion funnel) provide direct, quantified evidence for this exact domain. The primary gap is consumer marketplace / travel context (Airbnb's unique supply-demand two-sided marketplace) and a modest stretch on the "product narrative / marketing partnership" axis, which Riley has touched but not headlined.
recommendation_reason: 9 years of AI-powered discovery and search at consumer scale is a near-perfect domain match; strong quantified evidence across every core requirement.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping search, discovery, and personalization experiences at consumer scale — including a hybrid AI search platform serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV e-commerce platform. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers, data scientists, and UX teams to build AI-powered browsing experiences that measurably lift engagement, conversion, and retention.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 → $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The JD emphasizes "product narrative / marketing storytelling" and lifecycle campaign partnership — areas Riley has touched (AEO pipeline, conversational AI GTM, CRM marketing) but not headlined. In the resume and cover letter, explicitly frame the AEO pipeline launch (8x AI agent traffic, +40% conversion in 12 weeks) and the conversational AI chat experience as examples of crafting product narrative and driving cross-functional marketing alignment, not just engineering delivery.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley is a strong match on the core PM fundamentals Instacart requires: 9 years of consumer product experience, deep A/B testing and experimentation fluency, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — all explicitly called out as preferred qualifications. The main gap is the Health/nutrition domain, which is a specialized area Riley has no documented experience in; however, the role leans heavily on discovery, shopping UX, and personalization mechanics where Riley has direct, metrics-backed evidence. The zero-to-one framing is a mild risk — Riley's work at Samsung and Kolon involved significant new product launches, but those were within established platforms rather than true greenfield.
recommendation_reason: Strong overlap on consumer e-commerce, personalization, discovery, and experimentation; health domain gap is a preferred — not required — qualification and can be bridged with framing.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered discovery, personalization, and shopping experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon — serving 10M+ monthly users across global e-commerce platforms. Proven track record of launching zero-to-one consumer features with measurable impact on engagement, conversion, and retention through rigorous experimentation and deep user empathy.
top_bullets: Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy — increased PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reduced bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing with data scientists, engineers, and UX designers.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels — increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
gap_handling: The Health/nutrition domain is a preferred qualification, not a hard requirement. In your cover letter and interviews, frame your e-commerce discovery and dietary-filter-adjacent work (e.g., personalization, guided shopping flows, recommendation systems) as directly transferable. Emphasize your zero-to-one launches — the conversational AI chat and AEO pipeline at Samsung — as evidence of greenfield execution. If you have any personal connection to health/nutrition or wellness apps, briefly mention it to humanize the fit.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's search and discovery background at Samsung.com is a strong match — hybrid search, ranking optimization, behavioral signals, and search metrics are all directly relevant to Faire's Search Algorithms mandate. The B2B marketplace domain (Faire is wholesale/retail) aligns well with Riley's Amazon B2B and e-commerce history. The primary friction point is seniority: Faire is hiring a Staff PM, which is a stretch above Riley's current PM III / Senior PM target, and will attract candidates with 10+ years leading algorithm-heavy search teams at companies like Google, Amazon, or Pinterest. The 7+ year requirement is met, but "Staff" signals they want someone who can independently set a multi-year technical strategy from day one.
recommendation_reason: Domain and technical fit are excellent, but the Staff PM seniority level and hybrid NYC/SF office requirement are both meaningful hurdles for Riley.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale — from hybrid search ranking and query understanding to behavioral signal optimization — serving 10M+ monthly users. Deep B2B and marketplace intuition, built at Amazon and Samsung, with a track record of defining search metrics, running rigorous experimentation, and partnering with ML/data science to deliver measurable relevance and conversion gains.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff PM bar means Faire will expect a candidate who can independently architect a multi-year search algorithm strategy with minimal direction. In cover letter and interviews, Riley should proactively speak to owning the Samsung.com search vision end-to-end — roadmap, ranking strategy, metrics framework — and position the PM III title as a label mismatch relative to actual scope, not a reflection of the work's complexity.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing discipline, and experience shipping ML-powered features at Samsung and Amazon are strong signals of competence for this role. However, the role is explicitly focused on developer-platform / API growth — a B2B/PLG funnel archetype — and Riley's portfolio is weighted toward consumer e-commerce discovery and search, with only one B2B platform role (Amazon seller tools) that wasn't growth-focused. The gap in dedicated developer-platform or API growth product experience (onboarding funnels, console UX, usage-based billing, self-serve upgrade paths) is real and is likely to be the primary screen filter.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and growth fundamentals, but a meaningful gap in developer-platform / API-product experience means this is a competitive stretch requiring deliberate resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth through ML-powered search, discovery, and personalization products at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon — shipping at scale for 10M+ users and 2M+ B2B sellers. Proven track record leading full-funnel experimentation, activation optimization, and self-serve workflows that directly map to API platform growth levers including onboarding, conversion, and monetization.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — led self-serve workflow enhancements that saved $1.4M in operational cost and improved Ease-of-Use score by 380bps.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% through model optimization and iterative A/B testing.
gap_handling: The core gap is developer-platform / API growth experience. To address this: (1) In your cover letter, explicitly bridge your Amazon B2B seller-tools work to developer-platform thinking — self-serve workflows, activation funnels, and structured data pipelines are directly analogous. (2) Reframe your AEO pipeline and recommendation API bullets to foreground the API/integration surface, not just the outcome. (3) If you have any side projects, personal API usage, or opinions on Claude's developer onboarding, weave that into your narrative to demonstrate genuine platform-side empathy.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — spanning hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, recommendation APIs) — maps closely to the role's core focus on Copilot backend services and AI orchestration. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline directly speak to the RAG and agent orchestration requirements. However, the role is explicitly titled "Principal PM" (a level Riley marks as "Avoid") and leans heavily into developer-platform and backend infrastructure domains (API platforms, SID error logs, B2B developer tooling) that differ from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The 8+ years requirement is met, but the B2B developer/platform PM archetype is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal matches well, but the Principal title exceeds Riley's target seniority and the developer-platform/B2B backend focus is a meaningful archetype stretch from consumer e-commerce discovery.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and hybrid search pipelines to AEO metadata automation — that drive measurable engagement at scale. Proven track record orchestrating cross-functional AI product delivery across backend data pipelines, contextual personalization, and intelligent user assistance for audiences of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The Principal title and B2B developer-platform focus are the two honest gaps here. For the title, Riley can lean into scope (10M+ users, multi-market launches, cross-org influence) to justify Principal-level impact without claiming the title directly. For the developer/B2B gap, the Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing pipelines, multi-modal classification) is the strongest bridge — lead with it when positioning for this role, and frame Samsung's internal tooling and AEO pipeline work as developer-adjacent backend product ownership.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, personalization, conversational AI, and LLM-driven pipelines aligns strongly with the core technical domain — particularly the Samsung.com conversational AI, recommendation API, and hybrid search work. However, this is a Staff-level platform role explicitly requiring 9+ years focused on foundational platform infrastructure (RAG, retrieval optimization, context engineering, multi-surface extensibility), whereas Riley's experience skews toward consumer-facing product delivery rather than platform/infrastructure ownership. The customer support / community support domain is also a new vertical for Riley, adding a domain adjacency gap on top of the seniority stretch.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/personalization signal and 9 years of experience qualify Riley technically, but the Staff-level platform infrastructure scope and CS domain are meaningful stretches that make this a competitive, tailoring-required application.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping personalization systems, LLM-powered experiences, and hybrid search at consumer scale — including 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and $2B+ GMV at Kolon. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers on retrieval architectures, ranking models, and conversational AI to deliver measurable outcomes, with the technical fluency and cross-functional leadership to own full-stack personalization platforms.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform ownership depth — this role wants someone who has built foundational retrieval/RAG infrastructure that powers multiple downstream surfaces, not just shipped consumer-facing features on top of ML. In your cover letter and resume, lean into the AEO pipeline (structured data extraction at scale), recommendation API architecture, and Samsung search instrumentation strategy as the closest proxies. Be prepared in interviews to speak credibly about RAG tradeoffs, context window management, and latency/scalability considerations — even if you position your strength as the consumer experience layer built on top of that infrastructure.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — hybrid search, ranking, LLM pipelines, and agentic AEO systems — maps well to Lyft's AI Platform mission, and strong SQL/Tableau/BigQuery skills satisfy the data visualization requirement. However, this is a Staff PM role focused on an internal AI *platform* (developer tooling, multi-agent orchestration, build-vs-buy vendor management) rather than a consumer-facing AI product, which is a meaningful archetype gap. The explicit ask for hands-on agentic system design, multi-agent orchestration expertise, and SaaS contract/vendor management experience goes beyond what Riley's resume directly evidences, and Staff PM is a stretch above Riley's target seniority band.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML signal and tool credibility are real, but platform-PM archetype gap, Staff-level seniority stretch, and SF hybrid requirement reduce fit — tailor heavily before applying.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered systems — including agentic AEO pipelines, LLM-driven search, and multi-modal ML platforms — at Samsung and Amazon, with a track record of 8x traffic growth and 10M+ monthly user impact. Experienced defining AI platform roadmaps, instrumenting KPIs from raw data (SQL, BigQuery, Tableau), and influencing cross-functional adoption of new AI capabilities in large, fast-moving organizations.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, leveraging behavioral insights to increase search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform-PM experience: Lyft wants someone who has built internal AI tooling, managed platform adoption across teams, and handled vendor/SaaS contracts — Riley's background is almost entirely on end-user-facing products. To close this, Riley should reframe the AEO pipeline and Amazon listing tool as *platform plays* (internal APIs, multi-team adoption, developer tooling) and explicitly call out any cross-team enablement or internal evangelism work. If Riley has any experience with third-party AI vendor evaluations or build-vs-buy decisions (e.g., selecting ML tooling at Samsung), that should be surfaced prominently.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's profile is strong in AI search and discovery but the role is explicitly a Growth/Monetization PM position — focused on pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion, and subscription funnels — which is not Riley's primary archetype. While Riley has meaningful growth adjacent evidence (AOV lifts, retention, onboarding conversion at Kolon), there is no direct track record of owning monetization levers like pricing strategy, paywall optimization, or subscription upgrade funnels at scale. The domain (consumer AI / Claude.ai products) aligns well with Riley's target, and Anthropic's brand + visa sponsorship are strong positives, but the archetype gap — search/discovery PM vs. dedicated monetization/growth PM — is real and will show in a competitive slate.
recommendation_reason: Strong domain and AI alignment, but the monetization/subscription growth archetype is a genuine gap Riley must bridge with targeted tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable revenue and engagement growth across e-commerce and AI-powered product experiences, including search monetization, recommendation-driven AOV growth, and conversion funnel optimization at Samsung.com and a $2B GMV platform. Proven ability to partner with ML engineers and data scientists to ship high-impact AI features — now eager to bring that growth rigor to Anthropic's monetization strategy across Claude.ai and Claude Platform.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing — directly demonstrating funnel ownership and revenue impact.

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%, and designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to improve engagement for 10M+ monthly users — showing cross-functional scale and KPI ownership.
gap_handling: The core gap is that Riley has never explicitly owned a pricing/packaging strategy, paywall, or free-to-paid subscription funnel. To bridge this, Riley should: (1) reframe the Kolon onboarding + retention work as "monetization funnel" work with specific revenue outcomes; (2) call out any awareness of Claude's freemium/Pro model and articulate a point of view on subscription conversion in a cover letter; (3) consider adding a brief "Monetization & Growth" skills section to the resume that surfaces the AOV, conversion rate, and retention numbers front-and-center.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI product credentials and solid growth/conversion experience that maps reasonably well to this role's self-service AI and trial conversion goals. However, the core of this role — Help Center / knowledge base platform, support deflection, CustOps tooling, and agent-facing internal tools — is largely absent from Riley's background, which skews heavily toward search, discovery, and e-commerce. The "Multi-Brand Help Center" redesign and support enablement focus are materially different archetypes from Riley's AI Search and E-commerce DNA.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and growth signals are a partial fit, but the Help Center / CustOps support enablement core is a notable gap that requires deliberate framing to bridge.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years driving AI-powered self-service and discovery experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon—shipping conversational AI, hybrid search, and personalized recommendation systems for 10M+ monthly users. Adept at leading cross-functional teams from ambiguous objectives to measurable outcomes, with a track record of improving user engagement, conversion, and satisfaction at scale.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers, identifying key issues through 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys and partnering with 5 dependent teams to develop a solution
gap_handling: The biggest gap is direct Help Center / support deflection experience. Address this head-on in your cover letter by drawing a direct analogy: your conversational AI chat at Samsung was designed to deflect users from contacting support while resolving product questions — that's functionally identical to support deflection. Quantify it if possible. For agent tooling, lean on your Amazon seller-facing internal tool work as the closest proxy for internal-user product thinking.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. However, the role explicitly calls for a "former consumer founder" who spikes on 0-to-1 product creation — Riley's background is primarily 1-to-N optimization and feature scaling at established companies (Samsung, Amazon, Kolon), not greenfield consumer product invention or startup founding. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — including conversational AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of translating emerging model capabilities into products people love. Deeply hands-on across the full product lifecycle from vision and prototyping through scaled global launch, with a bias for creative, low-cost experimentation to validate product-market fit fast.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting product discovery and comparison for millions of users on Samsung.com

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — a 0-to-1 initiative taken from concept to production

Launched hybrid AI-powered search experience across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: Riley has no stated startup founding experience — the single biggest filter for this role. To address this, the cover letter should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat launches as 0-to-1 entrepreneurial bets driven by Riley's initiative, not top-down roadmap items. If Riley has any side projects, independent AI product experiments, or early-stage advisory work, those should be surfaced prominently. Leaning into hands-on prototyping with Claude or other AI tools (per the JD's "Claude Code" signal) would also help close the founder-proxy gap.

However, the role is fundamentally B2B SaaS (observability/analytics platform), and Riley's background skews heavily toward consumer e-commerce and retail search. More critically, the role requires deep domain knowledge of BI tooling, semantic layers, data warehouses, and the modern data stack (dbt, Snowflake, Looker, etc.) — areas not evidenced anywhere in the resume. The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial build, governance layer design, and BI-competitive landscape research are also outside Riley's demonstrated scope.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML product and SQL credentials are a partial match, but missing B2B SaaS depth and BI/data stack domain knowledge create meaningful gaps for a 0-to-1 product at this level.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving LLM-powered data products, search ranking systems, and structured data pipelines at scale — including conversational AI, AEO metadata pipelines, and multi-modal ML workflows at Samsung and Amazon. Experienced shipping 0-to-1 AI experiences that serve both technical and non-technical users, with a strong foundation in SQL, experimentation, and cross-functional product delivery in data-intensive environments.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories
gap_handling: Directly acknowledge the BI/data stack gap in a cover letter by framing your AEO metadata pipeline and structured catalog work as analogous to semantic layer design — you've built systems that normalize and govern structured data for AI consumption at scale. Mention any personal exposure to tools like dbt, Snowflake, or Looker even if exploratory. The "AI-pilled" signal is important — reference specific AI tools you use daily (Cursor, Claude, etc.) to show cultural fit with what they're looking for.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, search ranking, recommendation systems — that directly map to Okta's "AI-native experiences" mandate. However, this is a Principal PM role requiring 10+ years, and Riley has 9 years, creating a slight seniority mismatch; more critically, the domain is enterprise identity/IAM and internal productivity tooling (IT, HR, Finance, GTM workflows), which is meaningfully different from Riley's consumer e-commerce and search expertise. The "agentic systems," "enterprise compliance," and "responsible AI" nice-to-haves are also largely absent from Riley's background, and the role is clearly a stretch above Riley's target seniority band (Senior PM / PM III).
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/ML product evidence makes Riley competitive, but the enterprise identity domain and Principal-level seniority bar are notable gaps requiring deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years of experience shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and intelligent automation at scale — from hybrid search systems serving 10M+ users to production ML models driving measurable enterprise outcomes. Proven track record taking AI products from 0→1 to broad adoption, defining rigorous evaluation frameworks, and partnering cross-functionally to translate complex AI capabilities into simple, high-impact user experiences.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Defined an automated AEO strategy and LLM-driven pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.
gap_handling: Okta's domain is enterprise identity (IAM), with the role focused on AI for internal workflows (IT, HR, Finance, GTM) — not consumer e-commerce. In the resume and cover letter, lean hard into the platform/infrastructure angle: the AEO pipeline, recommendation API, and conversational AI work are your best proxies for "AI-native enterprise product" experience. Acknowledge the enterprise context gap indirectly by emphasizing cross-functional stakeholder leadership, 0→1 delivery, and scale. For the seniority gap, position your Samsung PM III title and scope (10M+ users, international expansion, executive alignment) as Principal-equivalent impact even if the title doesn't match exactly.

score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and AEO — that demonstrate genuine depth in AI product work, and the domain alignment with Anthropic's AI-forward mission is solid. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant. However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, supporting AI-driven product discovery for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience

However, this role is fundamentally a B2B SaaS / enterprise work-management platform role (Asana's Project & Task experience), which is a meaningful domain mismatch from Riley's AI search, e-commerce discovery, and consumer personalization background. The JD explicitly calls for experience in "complex data models and visualizations, human-centric UX, and B2B platform or enterprise experience" — Riley's Amazon B2B stint is relevant but relatively brief, and none of Riley's work involves productivity/collaboration tooling or complex relational data modeling. title: Senior Group Product Manager, Advertiser Optimization (Ads Marketplace)
score_rationale: This role is a Senior Group Product Manager — a people management position overseeing multiple PM teams within Reddit's Ads Marketplace (bidding, targeting, budget optimization, AI-driven ad recommendations). Riley's background is exclusively in e-commerce search, discovery, and personalization with zero demonstrated ads tech experience (no ad bidding, auction mechanics, ROAS, advertiser-facing tooling, or campaign management). The "Group PM" title explicitly requires managing other PMs, which Riley has no stated experience doing. While Riley has strong AI/ML and data credentials that are adjacent, the domain and seniority gaps are too wide to overcome without fabrication.
recommendation_reason: Two compounding hard gaps: zero ads-tech domain experience and no people management background — both are explicit minimum requirements for this Group PM role.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping ML-powered search, personalization, and discovery experiences at Samsung and Amazon, driving measurable growth for 10M+ monthly users. Experienced in defining product vision, leading cross-functional teams, and leveraging behavioral data and experimentation to optimize conversion and engagement at scale.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

gap_handling: The two gaps here are structural, not cosmetic. First, this is a Group PM role — Reddit explicitly needs someone who has hired and managed other PMs. Riley has no stated people management experience. Second, the entire product domain (ad auctions, bidding algorithms, budget pacing, advertiser ROI optimization) is outside Riley's experience base. Tailoring the resume won't bridge either gap. A better path: target IC Senior PM roles in Reddit's consumer/search/personalization orgs, or look for ads-adjacent roles (e.g., ads relevance, sponsored search/ranking) at companies like Pinterest or Microsoft Ads where Riley's search background is directly applicable.
